description: Authorization enforced only in the UI or via a client-supplied flag, so the API accepts forged privileged requests.
body: Failure mode: access is gated by hiding a button or reading a role from the request body / client state, while the server accepts the call regardless. Detection signals: the only 'check' is in frontend code or a request field; no server-side guard reads the authenticated principal; the endpoint returns success when called directly with a crafted role. Do not use UI gating, hidden fields, or client role flags as enforcement. Safer fallback: add a server-side check at the resource boundary using the session/token principal, plus a negative test calling the API directly as a non-privileged user.
